text.title: Visual recapture and out-of-band delivery
text.summary: For visual fixes, the final phase produces a focused before/after image pair (red outline before, green outline after) and posts them to a messaging delivery channel with explanatory captions in the user's working language.

section.title: Why focused, not full-page

rule.statement: Screenshots MUST be element-focused with roughly 200 pixels of context above and below the bug element. Width about 1400 px; height auto, capped around 1500 px.
rule.rationale: Full-page captures of long documents are unreadable when delivered through a messaging channel that downscales images. The reviewer's eye must land on the bug area in under one second.

section.reference: Playwright Python helper that locates an element, injects a CSS outline, and clips the screenshot to the element's neighbourhood.

list.item: BEFORE: render the pre-fix state and outline the buggy element in red (`#d50000`).
AFTER: render the post-fix state at the same scroll position and outline the same element in green (`#1b8a3a`).

Defensive fix (no visible diff in current data): outline the touched element with both red and green so the reviewer sees the changed area, and the caption explicitly says the diff will only manifest once corresponding data appears.

section.title: Caption convention
section.p: Captions are not decorative. Each caption explains, in the user's working language, what the bug was (BEFORE) or how the fix solves it (AFTER), in two or three sentences. Captions like ` before` are forbidden — they carry zero information the file name does not already convey.

antipattern.description: Adding a coloured banner with an English description at the top of the screenshot instead of outlining the actual element.
antipattern.reason: It hides the visual proof. The whole point is the reviewer's eye landing on the changed pixels, not on a label.

section.title: Delivery channel
section.p: The pattern is channel-agnostic: any messaging system that accepts images with captions is acceptable (chat bot, group channel, email with attachment, or even saving to a shared folder with an index update). Channel choice and credentials live in `reference/delivery-channel.md`. The orchestrator never hard-codes a vendor.

rule.statement: The recapture subagent MUST work in a dedicated worktree at the fix commit, not in the main working tree.
rule.rationale: Generating the BEFORE state requires temporarily reverting the fix files. Doing this in the main working tree corrupts user state and races with other agents.
